# Load the provided JSON files to inspect and search for the text.
import json, os, sys, re

quran_file = "/mnt/data/file-HHumqWkCbpLvx5yFcuNaEH-quranic_verses.json"
hadith_file = "/mnt/data/file-GTpsjEG93EG6BAuDqWqgAJ-six_hadith_books.json"

# Check files exist
print("Quran file exists:", os.path.exists(quran_file))
print("Hadith file exists:", os.path.exists(hadith_file))

# Load them
with open(quran_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    quran = json.load(f)
with open(hadith_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    hadith = json.load(f)

# Print counts and sample entries
print("Quran entries:", len(quran))
print("Hadith entries:", len(hadith))

# Search for exact text in hadith and quran
target = "لَا يُؤْمِنُ أَحَدُكُمْ حَتَّىٰ يُحِبَّ لِأَخِيهِ مَا يُحِبُّ لِنَفْسِهِ"
def find_in_list(lst, fieldnames):
    hits = []
    for i, item in enumerate(lst):
        for field in fieldnames:
            if field in item:
                if item[field] == target:
                    hits.append((i, field, item[field]))
    return hits

hits_q = find_in_list(quran, ["ayah_text"])
hits_h = find_in_list(hadith, ["hadithTxt", "Matn"])
len(hits_q), len(hits_h), hits_q[:5], hits_h[:5]

# Let's implement normalization and search functions, then search DB for best candidates.

import re
from collections import Counter

# Normalization function
arabic_diacritics = re.compile("""[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u0670\u06D6-\u06ED\u08D3-\u08E1]""")
# But to be safe, explicitly list common diacritics:
diacritics_chars = "ًٌٍَُِّْٰ"  # includes shadda, fathatan, dammatan, kasratan, sukun, superscript alif
# Build regex to remove known diacritics and tatweel
diacritics_re = re.compile(r'[\u064B-\u0652\u0670\u06D6-\u06ED\u0640]')  # include tatweel \u0640

def normalize_ar(text):
    if text is None:
        return ""
    s = str(text)
    # remove tashkeel (diacritics) and tatweel
    s = diacritics_re.sub("", s)
    # unify hamza forms to bare alif
    s = s.replace("أ", "ا").replace("إ", "ا").replace("آ", "ا")
    # replace hamza-on-ya and hamza-on-waw with ya/waw for normalization
    s = s.replace("ئ", "ي").replace("ؤ", "و").replace("ء", "")
    # normalize alef maqsura to ya
    s = s.replace("ى", "ي")
    # normalize te marbuta to ه? Many use ه for normalization; but that could reduce matches. We'll keep it as is or replace to ه
    s = s.replace("ة", "ه")
    # remove punctuation and symbols
    s = re.sub(r'[^\u0600-\u06FF\s]', ' ', s)
    # collapse spaces and strip
    s = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s).strip()
    # lowercase not applicable
    return s

# testing normalize function
sample = "لَا يُؤْمِنُ أَحَدُكُمْ حَتَّىٰ يُحِبَّ لِأَخِيهِ مَا يُحِبُّ لِنَفْسِهِ"
print("orig:", sample)
print("norm:", normalize_ar(sample))

# Precompute normalized fields and search
def prepare_candidates(quran, hadith):
    q_list = []
    for i, item in enumerate(quran):
        text = item.get("ayah_text", "")
        norm = normalize_ar(text)
        q_list.append((i, text, norm, item))
    h_list = []
    for i, item in enumerate(hadith):
        # hadith may have hadithTxt or Matn; use both if present
        for field in ("hadithTxt", "Matn"):
            if field in item and item[field]:
                text = item[field]
                norm = normalize_ar(text)
                h_list.append((i, field, text, norm, item))
    return q_list, h_list

q_list, h_list = prepare_candidates(quran, hadith)
print("Prepared counts:", len(q_list), len(h_list))

# Define similarity measures between two strings using normalization
def similarity_metrics(candidate_text, original_text):
    cand_norm = normalize_ar(candidate_text)
    orig_norm = normalize_ar(original_text)
    # token f1
    f1 = token_f1(cand_norm, orig_norm)
    # char LCS ratio: longest common substring length / len(orig_norm)
    _, lcs_len = longest_common_substring(cand_norm, orig_norm)
    lcs_ratio = lcs_len / max(len(orig_norm), 1)
    # also compute normalized edit distance ratio (1 - normalized lev distance)
    # implement simple Levenshtein
    def levenshtein(s, t):
        # classic DP
        if s == t:
            return 0
        if len(s) == 0:
            return len(t)
        if len(t) == 0:
            return len(s)
        v0 = list(range(len(t)+1))
        v1 = [0]*(len(t)+1)
        for i in range(len(s)):
            v1[0] = i+1
            for j in range(len(t)):
                cost = 0 if s[i] == t[j] else 1
                v1[j+1] = min(v1[j]+1, v0[j+1]+1, v0[j]+cost)
            v0, v1 = v1, v0
        return v0[len(t)]
    lev = levenshtein(cand_norm, orig_norm)
    lev_ratio = 1 - lev / max(len(orig_norm), len(cand_norm), 1)
    return {"token_f1": f1, "lcs_ratio": lcs_ratio, "lev_ratio": lev_ratio, "cand_norm": cand_norm, "orig_norm": orig_norm}

orig = "لَا يُؤْمِنُ أَحَدُكُمْ حَتَّىٰ يُحِبَّ لِأَخِيهِ مَا يُحِبُّ لِنَفْسِهِ"
c1 = list(unique_matns.keys())[0]
c2 = list(unique_matns.keys())[1]
sim1 = similarity_metrics(c1, orig)
sim2 = similarity_metrics(c2, orig)
sim1, sim2

for i, field, text, norm, item in [(i, fld, txt, nrm, itm) for (i, fld, txt, nrm) in substr_hits for itm in [None]]:
